---
title: "Maternal Mortality Dashboard" 
format: 
    dashboard:
      theme: styles.scss
jupyter: demo_file
---

```{python}
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import numpy as np
import itables

# load data
mmr_data = pd.read_csv("maternal_mortality_ratio_per_100000_live_births.csv")

# data wrangling
mmr_long = mmr_data.melt(id_vars=["geo","name"], var_name="year", value_name="maternal_rates") #wide to long

mmr_long = mmr_long.rename(columns={"name": "countries"})

mmr_long["year"] = pd.to_numeric(mmr_long["year"], errors="coerce") #convert years into numerics
mmr_long = mmr_long.dropna(subset=["maternal_rates"]) #drop any rows with missing data

mmr_recent = mmr_long[mmr_long["year"] >= 1990] #subset from 1990

latest_year = mmr_recent["year"].max()
latest = mmr_recent[mmr_recent["year"] == latest_year] #subset most recent year

# core metrics
global_avg_mmr = float(round(mmr_recent["maternal_rates"].mean(),1)) #average mortality rate
highest_country = (mmr_recent.groupby("countries")["maternal_rates"].mean().sort_values(ascending=False).index[0]) #worst country
lowest_country = (mmr_recent.groupby("countries")["maternal_rates"].mean().sort_values().index[0]) #least country

high_risk_countries = mmr_recent[mmr_recent["maternal_rates"] > 300]["countries"].nunique() 
countries_meeting_sdg = mmr_recent[mmr_recent["maternal_rates"] < 70]["countries"].nunique()
crisis_countries = mmr_recent[mmr_recent["maternal_rates"] > 500]["countries"].nunique()

# global trend
trend = mmr_recent.groupby("year")["maternal_rates"].mean().reset_index()

trend_chart = px.line(trend, x="year", y="maternal_rates", title="Global maternal mortality trend (1990–Present)")

#top ten countries
top10 = (mmr_recent.groupby("countries")["maternal_rates"].mean().sort_values(ascending=False).head(10).reset_index())

bar_top = px.bar(top10, x="maternal_rates", y="countries", title="Top 10 countries with highest maternal mortality")

# distribution
dist_chart = px.box(mmr_recent, y="maternal_rates", points= "all", title="Distribution of maternal mortality ratios globally", color_discrete_sequence=['#7f1d1d'])

# global maps
map_fig = px.choropleth(latest, locations="geo", color="maternal_rates", hover_name="countries",
    color_continuous_scale="Reds", title="Global maternal mortality map")

# high trend
top5 = (mmr_recent.groupby("countries")["maternal_rates"].mean().sort_values(ascending=False).head(5).index)

high_burden = mmr_recent[mmr_recent["countries"].isin(top5)]

trend_high = px.line(high_burden, x="year", y="maternal_rates", color="countries", title="Maternal mortality trends in highest burden countries")

# policy analysis
pivot = mmr_recent.pivot_table(index="countries", columns="year", values="maternal_rates")

pivot["reduction"] = pivot[1990] - pivot[2013]

top_improvement = pivot.sort_values("reduction", ascending=False).head(15).reset_index()

improvement_chart = px.bar(top_improvement, x="reduction", y="countries", title="Countries with largest reduction in maternal mortality")

#tables
table = latest.sort_values("maternal_rates",ascending=False).head(20)
```


# Global overview
## Row {height=25%}
::: {.valuebox icon="globe" color="#7F1D1D" title="Global Average MMR"}
`{python} global_avg_mmr`
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="exclamation-triangle" color="#DC2626" title="High Risk Countries (>300)"}
`{python} high_risk_countries` 
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="check-circle" color="#15803D" title="Countries Meeting SDG (<70)"}
`{python} countries_meeting_sdg`
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="activity" color="#7C3AED" title="Crisis Countries (>500)"}
`{python} crisis_countries`
:::

## Row {height=75%}
### Column {width="60%"}
```{python}
trend_chart
```

### Column {width="40%"}
```{python}
bar_top
```


# Global distribution
## Row {height=60%}
### Column {width="60%"}

```{python}
map_fig
```

### Column {width="40%"}

```{python}
dist_chart
```

## Row {height=40%}
### Column {width="60%"}

```{python}
trend_high
```

### Column {width="40%"}

## 
::: {.callout-note}
*Program Observations*: maternal mortality remains highly concentrated in a limited number of countries. Nations exceeding 300 deaths per 100,000 births represent priority health system strengthening zones. Global reductions since 1990 have been uneven, with fragile health systems showing slower progress.
:::

# Policy analysis
## row 
### Column {height=65%}

```{python}
improvement_chart
```

### Column {height=35%}
::: {.callout-note}
Key lessons from successful countries
evidence from Rwanda, Sierra Leone, Equitorial Guines and Eritrea shows rapid maternal mortality reduction.
:::

# About
This dashboard analyzes global maternal mortality trends using international health indicator datasets to support program planning, SDG monitoring, and maternal health policy decision-making.


```{python}
itables.show(table, caption="Top 20 countries with highest maternal mortality",classes="display nowrap compact", buttons=["csv" "excel", "Html5"])
```
